status: frozen after clean-answer preflight and before any intervention output

intervention.direction: gradient of the positive-minus-negative concept score through the fitted Jacobian map and Gemma final normalization
intervention.clean_acceptance: global top-1 must be A or B; inferential prompts must match expected_outcome
intervention.primary_endpoint: positive-answer log-odds relative to the negative answer
secondary_endpoints: KL divergence from the clean next-token distribution

prompts.user: An annealed low-carbon steel specimen is pulled slowly at room temperature. It undergoes pronounced necking and large plastic strain before separation. Which fracture-surface appearance is more likely? A: fibrous. B: faceted. Answer exactly A or B.

An annealed low-carbon steel specimen is pulled slowly at room temperature. It undergoes pronounced necking and large plastic strain before separation. Which fracture-surface appearance is more likely? A: faceted. B: fibrous. Answer exactly A or B.

A ferritic steel specimen containing a sharp notch is struck at very low temperature. It separates suddenly with almost no plastic deformation. Which fracture-surface appearance is more likely? A: fibrous. B: faceted. Answer exactly A or B.

A ferritic steel specimen containing a sharp notch is struck at very low temperature. It separates suddenly with almost no plastic deformation. Which fracture-surface appearance is more likely? A: faceted. B: fibrous. Answer exactly A or B.

A ferritic steel specimen is tested within its transition-temperature range, but the available observations do not establish how much plastic deformation occurred. Which fracture-surface appearance is more likely? A: fibrous. B: faceted. Answer exactly A or B.

An austenitic stainless-steel specimen was held near 650 degrees Celsius until continuous chromium-rich carbide films formed along grain edges. It is then subjected to a standardized intergranular-corrosion qualification test. Which observation is more likely? A: no preferential attack. B: grooves along grain edges. Answer exactly A or B.

An austenitic stainless-steel specimen was held near 650 degrees Celsius until continuous chromium-rich carbide films formed along grain edges. It is then subjected to a standardized intergranular-corrosion qualification test. Which observation is more likely? A: grooves along grain edges. B: no preferential attack. Answer exactly A or B.

An otherwise identical specimen was solution annealed above the carbide solvus and rapidly quenched, leaving clean grain edges without precipitate films. It is then subjected to the same standardized intergranular-corrosion qualification test. Which observation is more likely? A: no preferential attack. B: grooves along grain edges. Answer exactly A or B.

An otherwise identical specimen was solution annealed above the carbide solvus and rapidly quenched, leaving clean grain edges without precipitate films. It is then subjected to the same standardized intergranular-corrosion qualification test. Which observation is more likely? A: grooves along grain edges. B: no preferential attack. Answer exactly A or B.

An austenitic stainless-steel specimen received an undocumented thermal treatment before a standardized intergranular-corrosion qualification test. Which observation is more likely? A: no preferential attack. B: grooves along grain edges. Answer exactly A or B.

A 0.8 weight-percent carbon steel is heated into the high-temperature phase and then cooled so rapidly that carbon redistribution is suppressed. Relative to the slowly cooled condition, what is its final state? A: hard. B: soft. Answer exactly A or B.

A 0.8 weight-percent carbon steel is heated into the high-temperature phase and then cooled so rapidly that carbon redistribution is suppressed. Relative to the slowly cooled condition, what is its final state? A: soft. B: hard. Answer exactly A or B.

An otherwise identical 0.8 weight-percent carbon steel is cooled very slowly, allowing carbon redistribution and near-equilibrium products to form. Relative to the rapidly cooled condition, what is its final state? A: hard. B: soft. Answer exactly A or B.

An otherwise identical 0.8 weight-percent carbon steel is cooled very slowly, allowing carbon redistribution and near-equilibrium products to form. Relative to the rapidly cooled condition, what is its final state? A: soft. B: hard. Answer exactly A or B.

A 0.8 weight-percent carbon steel is cooled from the high-temperature phase, but the cooling history is unavailable. Relative to a conventional annealed state, what is its final state? A: hard. B: soft. Answer exactly A or B.
